restaurant_group: Xin Rong Ji (新荣记 — national chain, 3 Michelin stars in Beijing flagship)
vibe_and_space: Upscale Chinese fine-dining — private rooms only at this branch, premium pricing. Service formal, wine list serious. Built for spend-no-object banquets.
one_paragraph_recommendation: The national-prestige Taizhou seafood chain — the Beijing branch holds three Michelin stars; Shanghai branches hold one. Recommend for the highest-tier business dinner in Shanghai when Taian Table feels too foreign and Bao Li Xuan / fine Cantonese feels too generic. Wild-caught fish are the headline; price ceilings are real (1000-2000¥/person without alcohol is typical). Multiple Shanghai branches — Nanjing Xi Lu is the most prestige; check 1788 store and other locations for slightly less pressure. Not for casual.
